items.title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
items.description:
1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram	

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "Semiramis' Dream."
Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's Ten Total. But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: I would never go so far as to say there’s no place for AI in music (I’m a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I’m having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja’s “Semiramis’ Dream.”Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can’t quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024’s Ten Total. But the opener “Semiramis’ Dream” is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Benja’s incredible, obviously human voice front and center.You’d be forgiven for not immediately realizing that the song is the product of Suno. It lacks many of the dead giveaways. Even the most obviously AI part of the song — the choir of backup vocalists — is easily camouflaged amidst the hyperpop-influenced rapid-fire chops. Heavily processed and artificial-sounding vocals are a staple of many modern pop genres and, by leaning into that uncanniness, Benja is able to mask their origins.But Benja isn’t hiding. He told Hearing Things in an interview:My friend Patrick Holder did the beat. I recorded the vocals. Then we fed Suno the elements, settled on something, brought it out, edited it on Ableton, and then put it back into Suno. So when you look in Deezer, it marks the song as AI-generated. We’re not hiding it. We don’t feel any shame about it.Benja’s process certainly separates it from the rest of the Suno slop out there. He wrote the lyrics, sang the vocals, and worked with collaborators to perform the music. Then fed that into Suno, took what the AI spit out, brought it into Abelton, edited it, added more layers, fed that back into Suno… rinse repeat ad naseum. Benja estimates he did this a few hundred times for “Semiramis’ Dream.”While Benja professes to be agnostic about the tools he uses, he is under no illusion that using generative AI doesn’t come with ethical baggage. He acknowledges the damage it’s doing to the planet, but somewhat nihilistically says we can’t stop greedy corporations or put the AI genie back in the bottle: “The good guy doesn’t win this one,” he says.The only reason I’m using gen at all is because my visions are currently far beyond my means. People have been asking me, “Did you use gen AI for this EP?” I’m like, [sarcastically] “No, I hired a professional children’s choir.” What are we talking about? I haven’t paid rent last month, and I won’t pay rent this month. I don’t know where the fuck I’m gonna be.It’s hard not to empathize with Benja. Despite seeming to be on the verge of a breakout and earning a coveted Best New Music from Pitchfork for Ten Total, he’s a struggling artist who has endured periods of homelessness.Still, it’s also worth acknowledging that there is little on “Semiramis’s Dream” sonically that he couldn’t have achieved without AI. Sure, he couldn’t hire a children’s choir, but it’s not like the AI one found throughout the Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… is particularly convincing. A similar effect could be accomplished with some layered performances and smart vocal processing.1010Benja hasn’t converted me — I still remain deeply skeptical of AI art. But he has proven that, in the right hands, Suno doesn’t have to be a slop engine.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.titleZh: 我恨自己居然不讨厌这首用Suno做的歌
items.descriptionZh:
1010Benja 的最新 EP《Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose…》中的开场曲《Semiramis’ Dream》是一首令人意外的作品——它证明，即便在生成式 AI 泛滥的时代，人类依然可以驾驭技术而非被技术淹没。这首歌曲以丛林鼓节奏（jungle beat）炸裂开场，Benja 的人声清晰居中，听感完全不似常见的 Suno 生成歌曲那般廉价平庸。事实上，它几乎没有那些典型的“AI 味道”，即便最明显的 AI 痕迹——伴奏合唱团——也被淹没在 hyperpop 风格的快速切分处理中。大量后期处理和人造感强的声音是现代流行音乐的惯用手段，Benja 恰恰利用这种“诡异谷”效应，成功掩盖了 AI 的源头。

但 Benja 并未隐瞒。他在接受《Hearing Things》采访时详细披露了创作流程：先由朋友 Patrick Holder 制作伴奏节拍，Benja 亲自录制人声并创作歌词，然后将这些素材输入 Suno；AI 生成初始结果后，他将其导入 Ableton 进行剪辑、叠加新层次，再送回 Suno 循环处理。据他估算，仅《Semiramis’ Dream》一首歌就反复迭代了“几百次”。最终 Deezer 平台明确标注该歌曲为 AI 生成，但他们毫不遮掩，“我们不觉得有任何羞耻”。

这一流程与市面上多数“Suno 垃圾”截然不同。Benja 的核心创新在于：他并未让 AI 完全主导创作，而是将 AI 作为一个循环协作的“效果器”或“合作者”。人类写词、唱旋律、编曲，AI 负责生成变体和音色，人类再筛选、编辑、叠加，形成一个闭环。这种“人机共演”模式避免了纯提示词生成导致的空洞感。

从性能参数看：迭代次数约数百次，最终成品在 Pitchfork 上虽未获得像他 2024 年专辑《Ten Total》那样的“最佳新音乐”荣誉，但 EP 中其他三首均无法与《Semiramis’ Dream》匹敌。不过值得注意的是，从纯音色角度分析，这首歌中 AI 合唱团的部分其实并不比其他人工合成效果更“真实”——Benja 坦言他请不起真正的儿童合唱团（“我上个月没付房租，这个月也不会付”），但通过分层人声和智能处理，他其实完全可以用传统技术实现类似效果。换言之，AI 在此处的价值更多是降低成本和加速迭代，而非提供绝对不可替代的音色。

技术影响和应用场景方面，Benja 展示了生成式 AI 在独立音乐人手中的可能性：当资金匮乏时，AI 可以作为“穷人的管弦乐队”或“虚拟伴唱团”。他承认 AI 带来了伦理负担（环境破坏、版权争议），但以近乎虚无主义的态度表示“好人赢不了这一局”。这揭示了当下艺术创作者的困境——技术门槛降低与道德代价之间的拉扯。对于普通听众而言，这首作品提醒我们：AI 音乐的质量并不取决于工具本身，而取决于使用者的审美判断、手工打磨和诚实披露。虽然作者本人仍对 AI 艺术保持深度怀疑，但 Benja 至少证明了 Suno 不一定只能产出低质“泔水”——在正确的人手中，它也可以成为表达复杂情感的媒介。

items.title: AMD's next-gen 10-core 'Medusa Point' APU shows up on Geekbench again, with its best score yet — leaked SKU outpaces every other x86 mobile chip in the single-core test
items.description: AMD's next 10-core mobile part from the Medusa Point family is looking a lot faster than its previous two Gorgon Point and Strix Point SKUs, respectively. Early leaks keep highlighting an ever-improving part that has just benched its best score yet.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD is expected to refresh both its desktop and mobile lineups with Zen 6 next year. Ryzen AI 500 series, codenamed Medusa Point, will be the company's next-gen mobile family and we've already seen a 10-core part leak from it a couple of times. Now, that same SKU is back with another Geekbench listing, this time posting its best results that put it ahead of any mobile chip currently made by Intel or AMD (in one way). (Image credit: Future)AMD's upcoming chip ended up scoring 3,329 points in the single-core test and 16,555 points in the multi-core test. The Geekbench page once again shows the platform name as "AMD Plum-MDS1," which we've known to be associated with Medusa Point for a while now. The SKU is listed as "AMD Eng Sample 100-000001713-33_N," which is identical to how we saw in the last leak, basically confirming we're looking at the same chip.The single-core result is almost 5% higher while the multi-core score is about 9.7% higher compared to the numbers we saw previously. In fact, the single-core result is better than any mobile part on Geekbench apart from the new Snapdragon X2 Elite lineup. Compared to desktop equivalents, it's very close to the 9900X and the 9800X3D — two absolute desktop powerhouses carrying 12 cores and 8 cores, respectively.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Swipe to scroll horizontallySKUConfigSingle-Core ScoreDifferenceQualcomm X2 Elite X2E-90-100Mobile, 18C3,573100%AMD Ryzen 9 9900XDesktop, 12C3,33393%AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3DDesktop, 8C3,33393%AMD Ryzen 9 565? Mobile, 10C3,32993%AMD Ryzen 5 9600XDesktop, 6C3,31893%Intel Core i9-14900KSDesktop, 24C3,22690%Intel Core Ultra 9 285KDesktop, 24C3,19589%AMD Ryzen 9 565? (Previous)Mobile, 10C3,17489%The multi-core score puts this 10-core SKU right alongside the Ryzen AI Max+ 390, which is just a tier below the top-end AI Max+ 395 from Strix Halo. Since this is a 10-core part, we can infer it's the Ryzen AI 9 565 because the Ryzen AI 9 365 (from Strix Point) and Ryzen AI 9 465 (from Gorgon Point) share the same configs. The Ryzen AI 9 365 has an average score of 12,410, so Medusa Point's offering is 33% faster in this regard.Swipe to scroll horizontallySKUConfigMulti-Core ScoreDifferenceQualcomm X2 Elite X2E-90-100Mobile, 18C18,02678%AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3DDesktop, 16C22,16996%AMD Ryzen 9 9900XDesktop, 12C19,69686%AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3DDesktop, 8C18,31780%AMD Ryzen 9 565? Mobile, 10C16,55572%AMD Ryzen 5 9600XDesktop, 6C14,74264%Intel Core i9-14900KSDesktop, 24C23,008100%Intel Core Ultra 9 285KDesktop, 24C22,47298%AMD Ryzen 9 565? (Previous)Mobile, 10C15,09266%The Geekbench listing also reports the correct clock speeds this time — 2.0 GHz is still lasted as the base frequency, but the boost frequency is marked at 5.37 GHz, which makes a lot more sense. Moreover, the listing shows 10MB of L2 cache and 32MB of L3 cache while the Ryzen AI 9 465 had a combined cache tally of 34MB. You may like AMD's upcoming Zen 6 Medusa Point 10-core APU pops up on Geekbench AMD Ryzen AI 5 435G APU goes head-to-head with the Ryzen 5 8600G in early benchmarks AMD Ryzen AI Max+ PRO 495 APU could arrive with 192GB of unified memory Overall, this benchmark is an incredibly promising showing for Medusa Point. The Red Team's next-gen mobile lineup seems to benefit heavily from an architectural jump thanks to the Zen 6 silicon. Keep in mind that this is the company's mainstream mobile offering that's supposed to compete with Intel's Panther Lake, while Nova Lake mobile and AMD's own Gator Range (Ryzen 10000) will serve only as high-end CPUs. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: AMD下一代10核“Medusa Point”APU再次现身Geekbench，跑分创新高——泄露型号单核性能超越所有其他x86移动芯片
items.descriptionZh:
AMD下一代移动处理器Medusa Point（代号Ryzen AI 500系列）再次出现在Geekbench基准测试中，这次跑出了迄今为止最好的成绩。这颗10核工程样片（编号100-000001713-33_N）在单核测试中获得3329分，多核测试获得16555分，相比上一轮泄露结果分别提升约5%和9.7%。值得注意的是，其单核性能在所有移动处理器中仅次于高通最新的Snapdragon X2 Elite，甚至已经逼近桌面高端型号——与12核的Ryzen 9 9900X和8核的Ryzen 7 9800X3D（两者单核均为3333分）仅差4分，超过了Intel旗舰桌面处理器Core i9-14900KS（3226分）和Core Ultra 9 285K（3195分）。多核方面，这颗10核芯片与AMD自家的Strix Halo旗舰Ryzen AI Max+ 390相当，比上一代Strix Point的Ryzen AI 9 365（平均12410分）快33%。

该芯片基于Zen 6架构，从Geekbench信息看，基础频率为2.0 GHz，加速频率已达5.37 GHz，拥有10MB L2缓存和32MB L3缓存（合计42MB），高于上一代Ryzen AI 9 465的34MB。正如其平台名称"AMD Plum-MDS1"所暗示，Medusa Point是AMD面向主流市场的下一代移动APU系列，预计2025年推出，将与Intel的Panther Lake竞争，而更高端的Nova Lake及AMD自家的Gator Range（Ryzen 10000系列）则定位旗舰。这一成绩表明Zen 6架构带来了显著的IPC提升和频率优化，使移动芯片性能接近甚至超越部分桌面处理器。未来这些芯片将用于轻薄本、高性能游戏本和创作者笔记本，凭借强大的单核和多核性能，有望在高性能移动计算领域占据领先地位。

items.title: Scrying the AMD GFX1250 LLVM Tea Leaves
items.description: In just a few short days, AMD will be showing off their brand new MI400 series of Datacenter Accelerators at their Advancing AI event but before that event comes, we thought it would be fun to attempt to scry the tea leaves that are LLVM commits to see what we can ascertain about this next generation of AMD accelerator.
items.content: In just a few short days, AMD will be showing off their brand new MI400 series of Datacenter Accelerators at their Advancing AI event but before that event comes, we thought it would be fun to attempt to scry the tea leaves that are LLVM commits to see what we can ascertain about this next generation of AMD accelerator.
items.author: Aurora Nockert
items.descriptionZh: 再过短短几天，AMD就将在其"推进AI"活动上展示全新的MI400系列数据中心加速器。但在那之前，我们认为尝试从LLVM的提交记录中"占卜"一番，看看能窥见下一代AMD加速器的哪些端倪，会是一件很有趣的事。

history.title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
history.description:
1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram	

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "Semiramis' Dream."
Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's Ten Total. But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: I would never go so far as to say there’s no place for AI in music (I’m a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I’m having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja’s “Semiramis’ Dream.”Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can’t quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024’s Ten Total. But the opener “Semiramis’ Dream” is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Benja’s incredible, obviously human voice front and center.You’d be forgiven for not immediately realizing that the song is the product of Suno. It lacks many of the dead giveaways. Even the most obviously AI part of the song — the choir of backup vocalists — is easily camouflaged amidst the hyperpop-influenced rapid-fire chops. Heavily processed and artificial-sounding vocals are a staple of many modern pop genres and, by leaning into that uncanniness, Benja is able to mask their origins.But Benja isn’t hiding. He told Hearing Things in an interview:My friend Patrick Holder did the beat. I recorded the vocals. Then we fed Suno the elements, settled on something, brought it out, edited it on Ableton, and then put it back into Suno. So when you look in Deezer, it marks the song as AI-generated. We’re not hiding it. We don’t feel any shame about it.Benja’s process certainly separates it from the rest of the Suno slop out there. He wrote the lyrics, sang the vocals, and worked with collaborators to perform the music. Then fed that into Suno, took what the AI spit out, brought it into Abelton, edited it, added more layers, fed that back into Suno… rinse repeat ad naseum. Benja estimates he did this a few hundred times for “Semiramis’ Dream.”While Benja professes to be agnostic about the tools he uses, he is under no illusion that using generative AI doesn’t come with ethical baggage. He acknowledges the damage it’s doing to the planet, but somewhat nihilistically says we can’t stop greedy corporations or put the AI genie back in the bottle: “The good guy doesn’t win this one,” he says.The only reason I’m using gen at all is because my visions are currently far beyond my means. People have been asking me, “Did you use gen AI for this EP?” I’m like, [sarcastically] “No, I hired a professional children’s choir.” What are we talking about? I haven’t paid rent last month, and I won’t pay rent this month. I don’t know where the fuck I’m gonna be.It’s hard not to empathize with Benja. Despite seeming to be on the verge of a breakout and earning a coveted Best New Music from Pitchfork for Ten Total, he’s a struggling artist who has endured periods of homelessness.Still, it’s also worth acknowledging that there is little on “Semiramis’s Dream” sonically that he couldn’t have achieved without AI. Sure, he couldn’t hire a children’s choir, but it’s not like the AI one found throughout the Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… is particularly convincing. A similar effect could be accomplished with some layered performances and smart vocal processing.1010Benja hasn’t converted me — I still remain deeply skeptical of AI art. But he has proven that, in the right hands, Suno doesn’t have to be a slop engine.
history.author: Terrence O’Brien

技术影响和应用场景方面，Benja 展示了生成式 AI 在独立音乐人手中的可能性：当资金匮乏时，AI 可以作为“穷人的管弦乐队”或“虚拟伴唱团”。他承认 AI 带来了伦理负担（环境破坏、版权争议），但以近乎虚无主义的态度表示“好人赢不了这一局”。这揭示了当下艺术创作者的困境——技术门槛降低与道德代价之间的拉扯。对于普通听众而言，这首作品提醒我们：AI 音乐的质量并不取决于工具本身，而取决于使用者的审美判断、手工打磨和诚实披露。虽然作者本人仍对 AI 艺术保持深度怀疑，但 Benja 至少证明了 Suno 不一定只能产出低质“泔水”——在正确的人手中，它也可以成为表达复杂情感的媒介。

history.title: AMD's next-gen 10-core 'Medusa Point' APU shows up on Geekbench again, with its best score yet — leaked SKU outpaces every other x86 mobile chip in the single-core test
history.description: AMD's next 10-core mobile part from the Medusa Point family is looking a lot faster than its previous two Gorgon Point and Strix Point SKUs, respectively. Early leaks keep highlighting an ever-improving part that has just benched its best score yet.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD is expected to refresh both its desktop and mobile lineups with Zen 6 next year. Ryzen AI 500 series, codenamed Medusa Point, will be the company's next-gen mobile family and we've already seen a 10-core part leak from it a couple of times. Now, that same SKU is back with another Geekbench listing, this time posting its best results that put it ahead of any mobile chip currently made by Intel or AMD (in one way). (Image credit: Future)AMD's upcoming chip ended up scoring 3,329 points in the single-core test and 16,555 points in the multi-core test. The Geekbench page once again shows the platform name as "AMD Plum-MDS1," which we've known to be associated with Medusa Point for a while now. The SKU is listed as "AMD Eng Sample 100-000001713-33_N," which is identical to how we saw in the last leak, basically confirming we're looking at the same chip.The single-core result is almost 5% higher while the multi-core score is about 9.7% higher compared to the numbers we saw previously. In fact, the single-core result is better than any mobile part on Geekbench apart from the new Snapdragon X2 Elite lineup. Compared to desktop equivalents, it's very close to the 9900X and the 9800X3D — two absolute desktop powerhouses carrying 12 cores and 8 cores, respectively.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Swipe to scroll horizontallySKUConfigSingle-Core ScoreDifferenceQualcomm X2 Elite X2E-90-100Mobile, 18C3,573100%AMD Ryzen 9 9900XDesktop, 12C3,33393%AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3DDesktop, 8C3,33393%AMD Ryzen 9 565? Mobile, 10C3,32993%AMD Ryzen 5 9600XDesktop, 6C3,31893%Intel Core i9-14900KSDesktop, 24C3,22690%Intel Core Ultra 9 285KDesktop, 24C3,19589%AMD Ryzen 9 565? (Previous)Mobile, 10C3,17489%The multi-core score puts this 10-core SKU right alongside the Ryzen AI Max+ 390, which is just a tier below the top-end AI Max+ 395 from Strix Halo. Since this is a 10-core part, we can infer it's the Ryzen AI 9 565 because the Ryzen AI 9 365 (from Strix Point) and Ryzen AI 9 465 (from Gorgon Point) share the same configs. The Ryzen AI 9 365 has an average score of 12,410, so Medusa Point's offering is 33% faster in this regard.Swipe to scroll horizontallySKUConfigMulti-Core ScoreDifferenceQualcomm X2 Elite X2E-90-100Mobile, 18C18,02678%AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3DDesktop, 16C22,16996%AMD Ryzen 9 9900XDesktop, 12C19,69686%AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3DDesktop, 8C18,31780%AMD Ryzen 9 565? Mobile, 10C16,55572%AMD Ryzen 5 9600XDesktop, 6C14,74264%Intel Core i9-14900KSDesktop, 24C23,008100%Intel Core Ultra 9 285KDesktop, 24C22,47298%AMD Ryzen 9 565? (Previous)Mobile, 10C15,09266%The Geekbench listing also reports the correct clock speeds this time — 2.0 GHz is still lasted as the base frequency, but the boost frequency is marked at 5.37 GHz, which makes a lot more sense. Moreover, the listing shows 10MB of L2 cache and 32MB of L3 cache while the Ryzen AI 9 465 had a combined cache tally of 34MB. You may like AMD's upcoming Zen 6 Medusa Point 10-core APU pops up on Geekbench AMD Ryzen AI 5 435G APU goes head-to-head with the Ryzen 5 8600G in early benchmarks AMD Ryzen AI Max+ PRO 495 APU could arrive with 192GB of unified memory Overall, this benchmark is an incredibly promising showing for Medusa Point. The Red Team's next-gen mobile lineup seems to benefit heavily from an architectural jump thanks to the Zen 6 silicon. Keep in mind that this is the company's mainstream mobile offering that's supposed to compete with Intel's Panther Lake, while Nova Lake mobile and AMD's own Gator Range (Ryzen 10000) will serve only as high-end CPUs. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: AMD下一代10核“Medusa Point”APU再次现身Geekbench，跑分创新高——泄露型号单核性能超越所有其他x86移动芯片
history.descriptionZh:
AMD下一代移动处理器Medusa Point（代号Ryzen AI 500系列）再次出现在Geekbench基准测试中，这次跑出了迄今为止最好的成绩。这颗10核工程样片（编号100-000001713-33_N）在单核测试中获得3329分，多核测试获得16555分，相比上一轮泄露结果分别提升约5%和9.7%。值得注意的是，其单核性能在所有移动处理器中仅次于高通最新的Snapdragon X2 Elite，甚至已经逼近桌面高端型号——与12核的Ryzen 9 9900X和8核的Ryzen 7 9800X3D（两者单核均为3333分）仅差4分，超过了Intel旗舰桌面处理器Core i9-14900KS（3226分）和Core Ultra 9 285K（3195分）。多核方面，这颗10核芯片与AMD自家的Strix Halo旗舰Ryzen AI Max+ 390相当，比上一代Strix Point的Ryzen AI 9 365（平均12410分）快33%。

该芯片基于Zen 6架构，从Geekbench信息看，基础频率为2.0 GHz，加速频率已达5.37 GHz，拥有10MB L2缓存和32MB L3缓存（合计42MB），高于上一代Ryzen AI 9 465的34MB。正如其平台名称"AMD Plum-MDS1"所暗示，Medusa Point是AMD面向主流市场的下一代移动APU系列，预计2025年推出，将与Intel的Panther Lake竞争，而更高端的Nova Lake及AMD自家的Gator Range（Ryzen 10000系列）则定位旗舰。这一成绩表明Zen 6架构带来了显著的IPC提升和频率优化，使移动芯片性能接近甚至超越部分桌面处理器。未来这些芯片将用于轻薄本、高性能游戏本和创作者笔记本，凭借强大的单核和多核性能，有望在高性能移动计算领域占据领先地位。

history.title: Scrying the AMD GFX1250 LLVM Tea Leaves
history.description: In just a few short days, AMD will be showing off their brand new MI400 series of Datacenter Accelerators at their Advancing AI event but before that event comes, we thought it would be fun to attempt to scry the tea leaves that are LLVM commits to see what we can ascertain about this next generation of AMD accelerator.
history.content: In just a few short days, AMD will be showing off their brand new MI400 series of Datacenter Accelerators at their Advancing AI event but before that event comes, we thought it would be fun to attempt to scry the tea leaves that are LLVM commits to see what we can ascertain about this next generation of AMD accelerator.
history.author: Aurora Nockert
history.descriptionZh: 再过短短几天，AMD就将在其"推进AI"活动上展示全新的MI400系列数据中心加速器。但在那之前，我们认为尝试从LLVM的提交记录中"占卜"一番，看看能窥见下一代AMD加速器的哪些端倪，会是一件很有趣的事。
